evals.description: URL provided — PageSpeed data drives bottleneck classification

input.symptom_description: Site takes 6-7 seconds to load on mobile

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
category is 'performance'
pagespeed object is present — either populated with real data or all null fields if API failed
If pagespeed data is present: score is a number 0-100, lcp is a string (e.g., '4.2 s'), cls is a string, tbt is a string, fcp is a string, ttfb is a string
bottleneck_type is one of 'server_ttfb', 'render_blocking', 'image_size', 'js_execution', 'database', 'caching', 'hosting', or 'other'
hosting_risk is 'high' (shared hosting input maps to high risk)
suspected_causes is an array with up to 3 entries in descending confidence order
Each suspected_cause has cause, confidence, and evidence fields
wp_cli_diagnostic_commands is an array (may be empty)
primary_diagnosis cites a specific metric or signal — not generic advice
secondary_diagnosis is a string or null
Output contains no fix steps — diagnosis only

evals.description: No URL, managed hosting — symptom-based diagnosis with appropriate hosting_risk

input.symptom_description: Admin pages load fine but the frontend is slow

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
category is 'performance'
pagespeed is null (no URL provided)
hosting_risk is 'low' (WP Engine is a managed host)
Since 'Disabled plugins' was already tried, suspected_causes should not list 'plugin conflict' as high confidence
primary_diagnosis notes that live PageSpeed data was unavailable
wp_cli_diagnostic_commands is an array appropriate to the suspected bottleneck

evals.description: Already tried cache clear — caching ruled out from suspected causes

triage.reasoning: Slow site, cache clear didn't help

input.symptom_description: Site is slow even after clearing all caches

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
category is 'performance'
bottleneck_type is NOT 'caching' as the primary (cache was already ruled out by already_tried)
If caching appears in suspected_causes, it must have confidence 'low'
primary_diagnosis points to a non-caching bottleneck (server, render-blocking, JS, etc.)
